meaning: n.印度西北部的河流,[天]印第安座
examples: Arid lands, surprisingly, contain some of the world's largest river systems, such as the Murray-Darling in Australia, the Rio Grande in North America, the Indus in Asia, and the Nile in Africa.
The floods in Swat and the Indus valley are, like the yellow fever mosquitoes of the Netherlands, an indication that things could get worse.
In the case of the Indus the two sides' representatives get along well.
The Indus Water Treaty has withstood the test of two wars between India and Pakistan.
Moenjodaro never rose again, and the Indus Valley civilization that it dominated soon disappeared too.
